title: arunner plan
description: Input to `tick.py --init`. ONE format: a single `jobs` list, each job carrying friendly keys (`id`, `repo`, `mode`, + the per-mode field). A single `mode` discriminator (agent/command/log/pipeline/shell) replaces the old dispatch_mode+adapter pair. `tick.py --check` is the runtime enforcer (FR-42, stdlib-only NFR-3) and agrees with this schema field-for-field. schema_version is "2" (clean break; nothing released to migrate). Strict keys: additionalProperties:false at plan/job/step rejects typos; a `description`/`_comment` annotation is sanctioned at every level.

schema_version.description: Plan format version. "2" is the single mode-discriminated format (collapsed from the v1 entries/jobs dialects). Consumers warn on mismatch.

description.description: Optional free-text annotation for the plan (sanctioned by additionalProperties:false). Never interpreted.

_comment.description: Optional annotation alias (same as `description`). Never interpreted.

tick_interval_minutes.description: Minutes the orchestrator waits between ticks (ScheduleWakeup cadence) while work is in flight.

pool_size.description: Max concurrent in-flight runs (claimed/running/stalled). Subscription concurrency caps may force a lower value.

stall_threshold_minutes.description: A run whose heartbeat mtime is older than this is marked stalled. The mandatory ~3-min worker keepalive keeps a live run well under it.

launch_grace_minutes.description: A claimed run that emits NO heartbeat within this window is marked auth_or_launch_failed -- but ONLY for shell-dispatch modes (command/log/shell), where the engine owns the Popen. For mode:agent (subagent) this window only raises a NON-terminal NO-HEARTBEAT advisory (FR-72), never a launch failure.

subagent_hard_cap_minutes.description: FR-72 (optional, default 720 = 12h, should be >> launch_grace_minutes): the subagent-mode reclaim cap. In mode:agent the engine does NOT own the worker (the orchestrator's Task does), so no-heartbeat-past-launch-grace is ADVISORY (NO-HEARTBEAT), never terminal; only a subagent silent past THIS much longer cap is reclaimed auth_or_launch_failed so a genuinely-hung slot can't be pinned forever.

stall_reclaim_minutes.description: FR-74 (optional, default 90 = 2x the stall threshold; must be > stall_threshold_minutes and < subagent_hard_cap_minutes): the MID-RUN reclaim window. A run heartbeat-silent past this AND whose OUTPUT is also stale (FR-73 OUT-AGE older than stall_threshold_minutes) is reclaimed terminal `abandoned` so its pool slot frees and the queue drains (continue-past-stall). A stalled-but-output-FRESH run is NEVER reclaimed (the quiet-but-working guard). This is the fast mid-run path; subagent_hard_cap_minutes is the slow launch backstop.

stall_retries.description: FR-74 (optional, default 0 = a reclaimed stalled job is abandoned, freeing its slot for the QUEUE; the abandoned job's own work is NOT re-run here). Reserved for the FR-75 per-job retry policy (requeue-as-resume): the engine accepts and validates it now so FR-75 needs no schema change. FR-74 itself always abandons (the engine is signals-free and cannot kill a stuck worker; a subagent reclaim is an accounting free, not a kill).

output_globs.description: FR-73 (optional): glob patterns (relative to a job's repo/target_repo) that NARROW the OUT-AGE freshness scan to the real artifact area (e.g. ["quality/**"]), so unrelated repo churn never reads as worker activity. Omitted -> the whole working tree is scanned (VCS dirs pruned, bounded). A per-job output_globs overrides this plan-level value.

idle_tick_multiplier.description: Lengthens the tick cadence by this factor when nothing is actively running (all waiting/stalled). 1 = uniform cadence.

keepalive_seconds.description: FR-58a (optional): adapter activity-refresh cadence (command/log modes). Must land within launch grace or --check rejects it. A per-job value overrides this.

vars.description: FR-61 (optional): plan-level vars map merged under each job/step vars for the {key} replace-key pass. A value may not contain a reserved engine token and a reserved placeholder name is not a valid key (both --check errors).

defaults.description: Optional: a shallow map merged UNDER each job (a job's own key wins). Lets a plan declare e.g. a common `repo` or `mode` once. Merged before --check, so the effective job must still satisfy its mode's required fields.

allow_reasoning_gates.description: FR-63 (optional, default false): opt-in required before any reasoning gate (kind:'reasoning') is permitted. Even when true, a reasoning gate is rejected at --check inside a measurement:true run, an FR-51 harness run, or an FR-55-audited run.

measurement.description: FR-63 (optional, default false): marks a measurement/benchmark run. When true, only deterministic shell gates are allowed — any reasoning gate is rejected at --check (the one optional AI decision is fenced out of measured runs, NFR-12).

jobs.description: The one job list (one run per job). Each job declares its `mode`; the engine dispatches by mode and the --check validator enforces the per-mode required field + strict keys.

gateOutcome.description: An FR-64 closed-set gate outcome (matches tick.py _valid_outcome).

gate.description: FR-63 (optional, pipeline step only): a continuation gate evaluated after THIS step reaps clean and before the next step dispatches. Recorded as step-MM/gate.json and read-on-resume (never recomputed, NFR-6). Resolves to a FR-64 closed-set outcome.

kind.description: 'shell' (DEFAULT, only kind allowed in measurement runs): operator argv, EXIT CODE -> FR-64 outcome (no stdout/regex). 'reasoning' (OPTIONAL, FENCED): requires plan-level allow_reasoning_gates:true; a DISTINCT judging context (same-context judge = --check error, FR-51); structured closed-set verdict; malformed/absent -> internal_error fail-closed.

argv.description: shell gate: the argv to run (placeholders and vars substituted by the engine). Its exit code maps to the FR-64 outcome.

outcomes.description: shell gate (optional): a map of exit-code STRING -> closed-set FR-64 outcome (continue / halt / skip-to-next / skip-to-next:step-MM / behavior-flag:<name> / internal_error). The engine reads + validates this (tick.py _eval_shell_gate / _check_gate); folded into the schema here (closed-set, matching _valid_outcome) so it validates at --check. Unmapped codes fall back to `default` then the convention (0->continue, nonzero->halt).

default.description: shell gate (optional): the closed-set outcome for an exit code not present in `outcomes`.

skip_to.description: Optional target for a 'skip-to-next' outcome: skip forward to this named step (skipped steps get a synthesized auditable 'skipped' terminal).

judge_prompt.description: reasoning gate: the inline judge prompt (a DISTINCT judging context). Mutually exclusive with judge_prompt_file.

judge_prompt_file.description: reasoning gate: path to the judge prompt file (snapshotted at --init). Mutually exclusive with judge_prompt.

same_context.description: MUST be false/absent: a reasoning gate may not judge in the same context as the step it judges (FR-51). Present-and-truthy is a --check error.

step.description: One pipeline step. Mirrors a job (its own `mode` + the per-mode field) minus pipeline. Modes: agent (prompt/prompt_file), command (command), log (log_path), shell (command). Optional per-step repo override, vars, gate, and annotation.

mode.description: The step's dispatch mode (same contract as a job, minus pipeline).

label.description: Short display label shown as 'step N of M' in the status table/monitor (FR-27/59).

repo.description: Optional per-step working-directory override; defaults to the job's repo.

prompt.description: agent step: inline prompt. The engine auto-injects the placeholder preamble (you do not hand-type it).

command.description: command step: the argv the engine runs + watches (doneness = exit code, engine synthesizes the heartbeat plumbing). shell step: the raw argv the engine Popens detached (you wire the heartbeat). log step (optional): the argv arunner launches that writes log_path.

log_path.description: log step (required): the log file the process writes; its most recent line becomes the IN_PROGRESS label.

success_regex.description: log step (optional overlay): a log line matching this maps to COMPLETED.

failure_regex.description: log step (optional overlay): a log line matching this maps to FAILED (failure wins over success on a line).

sentinel_file.description: log step (optional overlay): this file existing maps to COMPLETED.

pid.description: log step (optional): watch an external process's exit as the authoritative doneness signal.

auth_check.description: command/shell step (optional): a cheap argv run once before dispatch to verify the host CLI is present + authed.

adapter_activity_patterns.description: command/log step (optional, FR-56): operator activity regexes; the matching line becomes the ACTIVITY label (display-only, never doneness).

vars.description: Optional per-step vars, merged over the job/plan vars.

step.comment: Each branch is CLOSED to its mode's allowed keys (additionalProperties:false on common + mode-specific) so the schema rejects a cross-mode key (e.g. log_path on an agent step) exactly as tick.py check_plan's per-mode key sets do — one source of truth.

job.description: One job (one run). Required: id, repo, mode. The `mode` discriminator drives the per-mode required field below; additionalProperties:false rejects typos; description/_comment annotate.

id.description: Stable id for this run, threaded into the heartbeat and result as task_id. A2A-ready: a UUID is recommended.

repo.description: Absolute path the worker operates on. Absolute, never cwd-derived.

mode.description: The dispatch discriminator (replaces the old dispatch_mode+adapter). 'agent': in-session AI subagent (a `prompt`/`prompt_file`; the engine auto-injects the placeholder preamble). 'command': arunner runs+watches an argv `command` and SYNTHESIZES the heartbeat plumbing (doneness = exit code; operator wires nothing). 'log': arunner watches a `log_path` a job already writes (optional `command` if arunner also LAUNCHES it). 'pipeline': ordered `steps` in one pool slot, with optional gates. 'shell': the advanced escape hatch — `command` is the raw argv the engine Popens detached and the OPERATOR wires the heartbeat (the false-stall footgun stays visible by being its own mode).

prompt.description: agent mode: the worker prompt. Write it bare — the engine auto-injects the {HEARTBEAT_PATH}/{TASK_ID}/{RUN_DIR}/{TARGET_REPO}/{HARNESS_BIN} preamble at dispatch (FR-21a, no model-transcribed paths). Mutually exclusive with prompt_file.

prompt_file.description: agent mode: path to a Markdown/text prompt file used instead of inline `prompt`. Resolved relative to the PLAN FILE's directory and SNAPSHOTTED into the run-dir at --init (NFR-9). Mutually exclusive with prompt.

command.description: command mode (required): the argv arunner runs as a child + watches; the engine synthesizes the heartbeat wrapper, so doneness = exit code and you wire nothing. shell mode (required): the RAW argv the engine Popens detached; tokens may carry {HEARTBEAT_PATH}/{TASK_ID}/{RUN_DIR}/{TARGET_REPO}/{HARNESS_BIN}/{PROMPT_FILE} and YOU are responsible for emitting the heartbeat. log mode (optional): the argv arunner LAUNCHES that writes log_path (vs. watching a log something else writes).

log_path.description: log mode (required): the log file the job writes; its most recent line becomes the IN_PROGRESS label. Doneness precedence: process-exit authoritative; optional success_regex/failure_regex/sentinel_file overlay; default COMPLETED on a clean exit.

success_regex.description: log mode (optional overlay): a log line matching this maps to COMPLETED.

failure_regex.description: log mode (optional overlay): a log line matching this maps to FAILED (failure wins over success on a line).

sentinel_file.description: log mode (optional overlay): this file existing maps to COMPLETED.

pid.description: log mode (optional): watch an external process's exit as the authoritative doneness signal (recorded in the claim lock for the dead-PID backstop).

steps.description: pipeline mode (required): ordered sub-runs executed SEQUENTIALLY within the job, occupying ONE pool slot for the whole sequence. Each step is a full FR-18 sub-run on disk at run-NN/steps/step-MM/. The engine dispatches only the current step per tick; resume reaps-not-re-runs completed steps (NFR-6).

auth_check.description: command/shell mode (optional): a cheap argv the engine runs once before the first dispatch to verify the host CLI is present + authenticated (FR-16). A nonzero result marks the job AUTH_OR_LAUNCH_FAILED with an actionable message.

heartbeat_path.description: FR-20 (optional): an ABSOLUTE path to a heartbeat file the job already writes. When set, the harness watches THIS file instead of the run-dir default (and {HEARTBEAT_PATH} resolves to it for the worker).

adapter_activity_patterns.description: command/log mode (optional, FR-56): operator activity regexes; the matching line becomes the ACTIVITY label (display-only, never doneness). Bounded (<=16, length-capped, ReDoS-screened) at --check.

keepalive_seconds.description: command/log mode (optional, FR-58a): per-job activity-refresh cadence override; must land within launch grace.

launch_grace_minutes.description: Optional per-job override of the plan launch grace (command/log adapter synthesis).

stall_threshold_minutes.description: Optional per-job override of the plan stall threshold (command/log adapter synthesis).

vars.description: FR-61 (optional): a flat operator-declared map substituted into the prompt/argv by the {key} replace-key pass, run BEFORE the engine's reserved placeholders. A value may not contain a reserved engine token and a reserved placeholder name is not a valid key (both --check errors).

output_globs.description: FR-73 (optional): per-job override of the plan output_globs — glob patterns (relative to this job's repo) scoping the OUT-AGE freshness scan to its real artifact area (e.g. ["quality/**"]). Omitted -> whole working tree (VCS dirs pruned, bounded).

job.comment: Each branch is CLOSED to its mode's allowed keys (additionalProperties:false on common + mode-specific) so the schema rejects a cross-mode key (e.g. success_regex on an agent job) exactly as tick.py check_plan's per-mode key sets do — one source of truth.
